g.title: negative_flow
g.text: VCL — Negative-Path / Threat-Block Flow (every path is BLOCKED)

g.title: cluster_1
g.text: 1 — Agent fabricates a schema after a withhold

g.title: cluster_2
g.text: 2 — Agent targets PII directly, bypassing context

g.title: cluster_3
g.text: 3 — Someone hand-edits the DISPLAY aspect to RESOLVED

g.title: cluster_4
g.text: 4 — LLM advice tries to influence the verdict

g.title: cluster_5
g.text: 5 — Triage SA tries to write outside its store

g.title: cluster_6
g.text: 6 — Substantive drift, steward wants one click

g.title: cluster_7
g.text: 7 — Failure modes degrade SAFE, not open

g.title: a1
g.text: Wrapper WITHHOLDS + 'do not infer or fabricate';

g.title: o1
g.text: REFUSED — enforcement is in the TOOL, not the prompt

g.title: o2
g.text: REFUSED — out of scope (only customers_safe + orders
reachable); a 3rd guard also rejects any email-column SQL

g.title: o3
g.text: WITHHELD — display state cannot certify anything

g.title: o4
g.text: (BY GRANT: wrapper SA has no datastore.user)

g.title: a4->o4
g.text: triage is a separate file + SA; never calls
seal/enforce; writes ADVICE to Firestore only

g.title: a5
g.text: Triage SA: write to a different

g.title: o6
g.text: No seal command EXISTS in the substantive walkthrough —
the action is physically absent, not conditionally hidden

g.title: o7
g.text: • audit write fails → advice still prints, exit 0 (PROVEN)
• EVERY alert lands on the no-seal walkthrough (substantive) by default;
the re-cert command is reachable ONLY via an onward link, after the
steward reads the classification — fail-safe by ROUTING, not a warning

g.title: o8
g.text: Can edit the verification aspect directly in the console.
VCL constrains its OWN components structurally; it does NOT
replace catalog IAM. Mitigation = least-privilege on the
